awards.title: LEAPS-MPS: Correlated Methods for Correlated Phases

papers.title: The influence of ripple on DC energy measurement and the improvement of wavelet threshold function filtering method
papers.abstract: DC energy has been applied in the construction of DC power grids, photovoltaic power generation, energy storage, and rapid charging of electric vehicles. The application of DC energy is of great significance for energy conservation, emission reduction, carbon reduction, and efficiency increase. In this paper, the reasons for the decrease in DC energy measurement accuracy caused by ripples in voltage and current are analyzed. The minimum requirements of ripples for DC energy meters are analyzed, and the traditional filtering method based on wavelet threshold is studied. An optimized and fast iterative wavelet threshold function was designed, the current signal of electric vehicle charging was selected, and the effectiveness of the algorithm proposed in this paper was verified through simulation.

papers.title: Application and Error Analysis of Differential Sampling for AC Power Measurement based on Quantum Voltage
papers.abstract: The quantum voltage standard based on the Josephson effect has been applied in AC power measurement. Differential sampling is the most promising method for measuring AC signals based on quantum technology. In this paper, we developed an AC power measurement system based on differential sampling and analyzed the system error sources through simulations and experiments. The system error comes from the sampling rate, step number of stepped waves, phase difference, and sampling time. The traceability of power to quantum voltage using differential sampling technology was achieved with an uncertainty of 10-6.

papers.title: Analysis of the temperature reference range for the verification of smart energy meter
papers.venue: 2022 IEEE 5th International Electrical and Energy Conference (CIEEC)
papers.abstract: A structured model of smart energy meter is firstly established in this paper, and the main factor that leads to smart energy meter errors is analyzed, that is the gradual change of reference voltage of AD converter with temperature. Then, the mathematical models of CMOS bandgap reference and AD converter affected by temperature are given. And by using the correlation analysis, the influence and relationship between the reference voltage of smart energy meter and temperature is investigated. The relationship curve between the error change of smart energy meter and the temperature change with time is given through experiments. Finally, according to the accuracy level of the tested smart energy meter, the suggestions on reference temperature conditions of smart energy meters is proposed.

papers.title: Research on dynamic error of digital input electricity meters for energy internet
papers.venue: 2022 IEEE 5th International Electrical and Energy Conference (CIEEC)
papers.abstract: Under the background of energy internet, the dynamic characteristics of power grid are prominent. Aiming at the dynamic error problem of digital input electricity meters widely used in smart substations, a dynamic error test experiment was designed and conducted in this paper, and the causes of dynamic errors were analyzed. The results show that the digital input electricity meter has advantages in measurement accuracy compared with the analog electricity meter. The analysis process can effectively guide the relevant manufacturers to take corresponding measures to improve the metering performance of digital input electricity meters.

papers.title: Design of a High-precision 5V/10V DC Voltage Reference Source
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a self-developed DC voltage source, which uses the Zener reference LTZ1000 as the voltage reference element. Selecting high precise resistors and using appropriate measures to insulate and isolate, the short—time relative stability of 7V output voltage provided by LTZ1000 is 1.5×10-8. The voltage of 10V is increased up by a network, composed by high precise resistors with the same temperature coefficient, and its short—time relative stability is 2.2×10-8. The voltage of 5V is divided by a network, composed by high precise resistors with the same temperature coefficient, and its short—time relative stability is 3.6×10-7. The circuit is suitable for signal acquisition, voltage calibration and other applications.

papers.title: The Impact of Sampling Period Change on Dynamic Phasor Traceability Algorithm Using Least Squares
papers.venue: IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environment
papers.abstract: In recent years, the electrical signal in Power System becomes more and more complex because of large-scale applications of power electronic equipment. To accurately trace the dynamic phasor, the algorithm based on least squares is used. It is important to develop the dynamic phasor traceability algorithm with high accuracy, good real-time performance and satisfied dynamic property. In this study, the applicability of least squares in dynamic phasor traceability is studied. The relationship between the sampling period and the fundamental period is deduced based on the formula. The accuracy is verified when the sampling period is three quarters of fundamental period. The simulation results show that when the sampling period is three quarters of fundamental period, the frequency response amplitude of this algorithm is flat near the fundamental frequency. Thus, the dynamic phasor can be accurately traced.

papers.title: An Interharmonics Traceability Method Based on Thermal Converters
papers.abstract: Product safety is essential for the power grid. However, the application of nonlinear power electronic equipment could cause safety problems in the power grid. The application of nonlinear power electronic equipment in the field of wind energy have led to continuous interharmonics in the power grid. The traditional harmonic analysis method is based on the Fourier transform. Nevertheless, the Fourier transform method could cause obvious spectral leakage and fence effects, resulting in large errors in the analysis results. To solve the problem that traditional methods cannot accurately trace the interharmonic signal to DC voltage, a traceability method of the interharmonic signal based on thermal converters is proposed. A traceability device of the interharmonic signal based on thermal converters is developed. With the novel device, the interharmonic signal is converted into thermal signal. Then the thermal signal is converted into electrical signal. The total effective value of the interharmonics signal is obtained by equivalent calculating. Finally, the interharmonics signal is traced to DC voltage. The results show that the present method can accurately trace the interharmonic signal. This novel method is useful for improving product safety in the power grid.
